agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order lifecycle management — specifically the cancellation policy governing when and whether a placed order can be cancelled before fulfillment. Three stakeholder groups (Product, Finance, Operations) have provided conflicting constraints on cancellation eligibility.","bounded_context":"Order Management / Order Lifecycle","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A customer's request to purchase goods, tracked through a lifecycle from placement to shipment."},{"term":"Cancellation","definition":"The act of voiding an order, reversing its commitments. The exact eligibility window is currently in dispute."},{"term":"Invoice","definition":"A financial document that locks in pricing and creates a receivable. Finance considers invoicing irreversible."},{"term":"Shipment","definition":"The physical dispatch of goods to the customer. Product considers this the point after which cancellation is impossible."},{"term":"Order Placement","definition":"The moment a customer confirms an order. Operations states invoicing occurs at this point."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer places an order and immediately requests cancellation","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — Under PM's rule, this is allowed (not yet shipped). Under Finance + Ops rules, this is forbidden (already invoiced at placement). This is the core contradiction."},{"scenario":"Order is placed, not yet shipped, customer wants to cancel","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — Same contradiction applies to the entire window between placement and shipment."},{"scenario":"Order placed but invoice generation fails or is delayed","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — If invoicing is assumed to be synchronous with placement but fails, does a cancellation window open? Operations' rule implies invoicing is immediate, but systems can fail."},{"scenario":"Partial shipment scenario","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — If an order has multiple line items and some have shipped, cancellation eligibility of remaining items is unspecified."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"High. If cancellation is allowed post-invoice, Finance must support credit notes or invoice reversal — contradicting their stated constraint that invoicing is irreversible. If cancellation is blocked at placement, customer satisfaction and conversion may drop significantly.","operational":"High. The resolution determines whether Operations needs to support invoice voiding/credit workflows, or whether Customer Support must reject all cancellation requests post-placement.","user":"Critical. If the Finance/Ops interpretation wins, customers effectively cannot cancel any order ever — a severe UX degradation compared to industry norms. If the PM interpretation wins, Finance must redesign their invoicing model.","risk":"Implementing either interpretation without resolving the contradiction guarantees a production incident or stakeholder escalation. Building to PM spec breaks Finance's invariant; building to Finance spec makes the cancellation feature useless.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Cannot cancel orders at all if Finance/Ops rules dominate, leading to chargebacks, complaints, and lost trust."},{"group":"Finance Team","impact":"Must support invoice reversal or credit notes if PM's rule dominates — contradicts their stated irreversibility constraint."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"May need to decouple invoicing from placement, or introduce a pre-invoice hold period, depending on resolution."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will bear the brunt of complaints regardless — needs a clear, defensible policy to communicate to customers."},{"group":"Product Management","impact":"Feature as specified is undeliverable without resolving the invoicing contradiction."}]},"confidence":{"level":35,"reasoning":"Three authoritative stakeholder groups have provided requirements that form a logical impossibility. PM says cancellable before shipment. Finance says not cancellable after invoicing. Ops says invoicing happens at placement. Transitively: orders are never cancellable — which directly contradicts PM's requirement. No amount of analysis can resolve this without a stakeholder decision. Confidence is well below 50 due to a hard contradiction.","high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction is real and not a matter of interpretation — the three statements are logically incompatible.","Invoicing and cancellation eligibility are tightly coupled and must be resolved together."],"low_confidence_areas":["Which stakeholder's constraint should yield — this is a business decision, not a technical one.","Whether 'invoiced' in Finance's language means the same thing as 'invoiced at placement' in Ops' language.","Whether Finance would accept a credit note or reversal as distinct from 'cancelling an invoice'."],"assumptions":["All three statements are accurately representing current policy intent, not aspirational goals.","'Invoice' means the same thing across Finance and Operations contexts.","There is no existing credit note or reversal process that Finance considers distinct from cancellation."]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Logical impossibility across three requirements: (1) PM says orders are cancellable before shipment. (2) Finance says invoiced orders are not cancellable. (3) Ops says orders are invoiced at placement. These three rules together mean orders are never cancellable after placement, which nullifies the PM's cancellation feature entirely. The three constraints cannot all be true simultaneously.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Finance Team","Operations Team","Customers","Customer Support"],"options":["A: Finance yields — introduce credit notes or invoice reversal as a mechanism distinct from 'cancelling an invoice', allowing order cancellation while preserving financial audit trail.","B: Operations yields — decouple invoicing from placement. Introduce a hold period (e.g., invoice only at pick/pack or shipment), preserving both PM's cancellation window and Finance's irreversibility rule.","C: PM yields — accept that cancellation is not possible post-placement. Reframe the feature as 'order modification' or 'return-before-shipment' with a refund workflow rather than cancellation.","D: Hybrid — invoice at placement for pricing lock but introduce a 'void invoice' status that is permitted only before shipment, with Finance explicitly approving this as a narrow exception to irreversibility."],"recommendation":"Option B (decouple invoicing from placement) is the most common industry pattern. Price can be locked via a 'price hold' or 'quote lock' mechanism without generating a formal invoice. This preserves Finance's invoicing irreversibility, gives PM the cancellation window, and aligns with standard e-commerce and logistics practices. However, this is a business decision that requires all three stakeholders in a room."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot implement order cancellation feature as specified","business_rule_broken":"The combination of all three stated rules creates a logical impossibility — no valid cancellation window exists. Implementing any subset of these rules will violate at least one stakeholder's stated constraint."}]}
